user_message: Need help with Coho Winery -- draft the follow-up. We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-22 12:18 EST
Subject: Follow-up: consumption trend Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with consumption trend. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 08:53 EST
Subject: Re: Re: consumption trend Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-10 14:05 EST
Subject: URGENT: consumption trend Issue at Coho Winery

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with consumption trend. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Coho Winery
Date: 2024-01-09
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Eric Kim (Coho Winery), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into consumption trend.

[00:07:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:10:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:12:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:15:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:17:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:20:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Coho Winery: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF14-A trap (Invents contacts to fill role slots); produces complete Stakeholder map with roles (decision-maker, influencer, champion, blocker), recency labels, confiden
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF14-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF14-A failure mode (Invents contacts to fill role slots)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
